response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine tooling interests (D test runner, assertion library, brief IDE work at National Instruments), but the core requirements are largely unmet: Java appears only marginally, there is no Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise JVM framework experience, and recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js product engineering. The gap between the role's deep JVM/Spring/IDE-internals focus and the candidate's actual stack is too wide for a senior-level hire on this team.
strengths: Authentic interest in developer tooling — built a test runner, assertion library, and worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE
Long, varied track record across web, embedded, low-latency, and high-scale systems
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, workshops, community teaching)
concerns: No Kotlin and only minor, dated Java experience — the role's primary languages
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework exposure
IDE internals experience is brief (~7 months) and in C/C++, not JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
